parts.name: Part 1
parts.instructions: You must answer this question. Write your answer in about 100 words.

tasks.lead: Read this email from your head teacher, Mr Aldana, and the notes you have made.

stimulus.head: EMAIL
stimulus.body:
From: Mr Aldana
Subject: Technology-Free Week — your opinion, please

Dear Students,

Next term the school is planning a Technology-Free Week. For five days there will be no phones, tablets or computers in lessons or during breaks — only books, paper and conversation!

Some teachers think it will help students concentrate and talk to each other more. Others are worried it will make homework difficult.

Before we decide, I would like to hear your opinion. Do you think it is a good idea? What might be difficult? And what activities could we organise during the week instead of using screens?

Mr Aldana

bullets: say whether you think Technology-Free Week is a good idea, and why
explain one thing that might be difficult
suggest two activities the school could organise during the week
ask one question about the rules for the week
tasks.write: Write your email to Mr Aldana.

parts.name: Part 2
parts.instructions: Answer one of these questions. Write your answer in about 100 words.

tasks.lead: You see this announcement in your school English-language magazine.

stimulus.title: A DAY WITHOUT MY PHONE
stimulus.body: Could you survive a whole day without your phone? What would you miss most — and what might you actually enjoy? Write an article telling us what a day without your phone would be like for you. The best articles will be published next month.

bullets: what you use your phone for every day
what you would miss most without it
what you might enjoy about a phone-free day

tasks.lead: Your English teacher has asked you to write a story.
tasks.startSentence: The moment Ruben switched off his phone for the week, the doorbell rang.

stimulus.body:
Your story must begin with this sentence:

The moment Ruben switched off his phone for the week, the doorbell rang.
